source: Low HCT is a common finding in T1D due to chronic inflammation and impaired erythropoiesis.
Anemia is a common finding in T1D due to chronic inflammation and impaired erythropoiesis.
Low MCH can be seen in T1D due to iron deficiency or impaired erythropoiesis.
Low MCH can be seen in T1D due to iron deficiency or impaired erythropoiesis.
Microcytosis (low MCV) can be seen in T1D due to iron deficiency or impaired erythropoiesis.
Thrombocytosis can be seen in T1D due to increased platelet production.
Low RBC count is a common finding in T1D due to chronic inflammation and impaired erythropoiesis.
Elevated RDW can indicate increased red blood cell production variability, which can be seen in early T1D.
Elevated WBC count can be a sign of infection or inflammation, which can be associated with T1D.
